nativeRange.habitat: Found on rock and sand bottoms and along the eroding edges of weed beds, typically at depths of 1–12 m. The species is associated with reef environments across its range.

diet.notes: Has been documented eating a variety of meaty foods including marine fish flesh, crustacean flesh, mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, krill, and zooplankton. Generally advised to offer small meaty items a couple of times daily.

summary: The Dusky Jawfish is a peaceful, burrowing saltwater fish native to the Caribbean and western Atlantic, generally considered reef-safe and suitable for beginner hobbyists. It spends much of its time at the entrance to a self-made burrow and is well known for jumping, making a tightly sealed aquarium cover essential.
careNotes: The Dusky Jawfish tends to inhabit the bottom of the aquarium, spending much of its time hovering at the entrance to a self-constructed burrow. A substrate depth of at least 7–10 cm of mixed coral sand and rubble is generally advised to allow natural burrowing behavior. A tightly sealed lid is strongly recommended, as the Dusky Jawfish has been reported to jump — particularly in the early weeks after introduction. Multiple individuals can sometimes be housed together in larger aquariums where each fish is able to establish its own territory; aggression between conspecifics is typically limited to territorial disputes near burrows. The species is commonly considered reef-safe. Moderate lighting has been reported to suit this species well, and gradual lighting transitions tend to reduce startling.
breedingNotes: Paternal mouthbrooding has been documented in this species — the male incubates and aerates the fertilized egg mass in his mouth. Successful captive spawning has been reported. Breeding difficulty in captivity is generally considered moderate due to the difficulty of obtaining a compatible pair and ensuring adequate space for territory establishment.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (14 cm max TL), native range (western Atlantic: southern Florida to northern South America), and preferred temperature range (25.5–28.2°C). Taxonomy: family Opistognathidae.

additional.name: FishyWiki
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (9.4 cm), minimum tank (113 L), salinity SG 1.020–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12.

additional.name: TankFacts
additional.notes: Provided additional water parameter data: temperature 76–80°F (24.4–26.7°C), SG 1.023–1.026, pH 7.8–8.4, dKH 8–12. Used in temperature and salinity averaging.

additional.name: Reeflex
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (12–14 cm), minimum tank (200 L), temperature (up to 26°C). Notes species as rare in trade and confirms mouthbrooding behavior.

fish.breedingNotes: The Dusky Jawfish is a paternal mouthbrooder, meaning males incubate the fertilized egg mass inside the mouth until hatching. Captive spawning has been documented, though breeding is generally considered moderately challenging due to the difficulty of obtaining a compatible pair and providing adequate space for territory establishment.
